You can create a black, lead flail using your Pact of the Blade feature. The flail’s head is sculpted to resemble a pair of grasping tentacles. The weapon has the reach property. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 necrotic damage to the target per spell level, and you can reduce the creature’s speed to 0 feet until the end of your next turn.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Cloak of Baalzebul (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron
As a bonus action, you can conjure a swarm of buzzing flies around you. The swarm grants you advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks but disadvantage on all other Charisma checks. In addition, a creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of you takes poison damage equal to your Charisma modifier. You can dismiss the swarm with another bonus action.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Curse Bringer (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Hexblade patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a greatsword forged from silver, with black runes etched into its blade, using your Pact of the Blade feature. If you reduce a target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse to 0 hit points with this sword, you can immediately change the target of the curse to a different creature. This change doesn’t extend the curse’s duration.
When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 slashing damage to the target per spell level, and you can reduce the creature’s speed to 0 feet until the end of your next turn.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Kiss of Mephistopheles (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, the Fiend patron, eldritch blast cantrip
You can channel the fires of Mephistopheles through your eldritch blast. When you hit a creature with that cantrip, you can cast fireball as a bonus action using a warlock spell slot. However, the spell must be centered on a creature you hit with eldritch blast.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Frost Lance (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron, eldritch blast cantrip
You draw on the gifts of the Prince of Frost to trap your enemies in ice. When you hit a creature with your eldritch blast cantrip one or more times on your turn, you can reduce that creature’s speed by 10 feet until the end of your next turn.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Grasp of Hadar (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron, eldritch blast cantrip
Once during your turn when you hit a creature with your eldritch blast cantrip, you can move
                    that creature in a straight line 10 feet closer to yourself.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Green Lord’s Gift (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
The Green Lord oversees a verdant realm of everlasting summer. Your soul is linked to his power. Whenever you regain hit points, you treat any dice rolled to determine the hit points you regain as having rolled their maximum value.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Improved Pact Weapon (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, Pact of the Blade feature
Any weapon you create using your Pact of the Blade feature is a +1 weapon. This invocation doesn’t affect a magic weapon you transformed into your pact weapon.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mace of Dispater (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron, Pact of the Blade feature
When you create your pact weapon as a mace, it manifests as an iron mace forged in Dis, the second of the Nine Hells. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 force damage to the target per spell level, and you can knock the target prone if it is Huge or smaller.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Moon Bow (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a longbow using your Pact of the Blade feature. When you draw back its string and fire, it creates an arrow of white wood, which vanishes after 1 minute. You have advantage on attack rolls against lycanthropes with the bow. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 radiant damage to the target per spell level.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Path of the Seeker (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Seeker patron
The Seeker bids you to travel in search of knowledge, and little can prevent you from waling this path. You ignore difficult terrain, have advantage on all checks to escape a grapple, manacles, or rope bindings, and advantage on saving throws against being paralyzed.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Raven Queen’s Blessing (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: Raven Queen patron, eldritch blast cantrip
When you score a critical hit with your eldritch blast cantrip, pick yourself or an ally you can see within 30 feet of you. The chosen creature can immediately expend a Hit Die to regain hit points equal to the roll + the creature’s Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hit point).

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Sea Twins’ Gift (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
The Sea Twins rule seas in the Feywild. Their gift allows you to travel through water with ease. You can breathe underwater, and you gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
You can also cast water breathing using a warlock spell slot. Once you cast it using this invocation, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Seeker’s Speech (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Seeker patron
Your quest for knowledge allows you to master any spoken language. When you complete a long rest, you can pick two languages. You gain the ability to speak, read, and write the chosen languages until you finish your next long rest.

5.name: Eldritch Invocation: Tomb of Levistus (UA)
5.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron
As a reaction when you take damage, you can entomb yourself in ice, which melts away at the end of your next turn. You gain 10 temporary hit points per warlock level, which take as much of the triggering damage as possible. You also gain vulnerability to fire damage, your speed drops to 0, and you are incapacitated. All of these effects end when the ice melts.
Once you use this invocation, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

6.name: The Archfey: Misty Escape
6.description:
Starting at 6th level, you can vanish in a puff of mist in response to harm. When you take damage, you can use your reaction to turn invisible and teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You remain invisible until the start of your next turn or until you attack or cast a spell.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

6.name: The Fiend: Dark One's Own Luck
6.description:
Starting at 6th level, you can call on your patron to alter fate in your favor. When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can use this feature to add a d10 to your roll. You can do so after seeing the initial roll but before any of the roll's effects occur.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest

6.name: The Great Old One: Entropic Ward
6.description:
At 6th level, you learn to magically ward yourself against attack and to turn an enemy's failed strike into good luck for yourself. When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on that roll. If the attack misses you, your next attack roll against the creature has advantage if you make it before the end of your next turn.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

6.name: The Undying: Defy Death
6.description:
Starting at 6th level, you can give yourself vitality when you cheat death or when you help someone else cheat it. You can regain hit points equal to 1d8 + your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hit point) when you succeed on a death saving throw or when you stabilize a creature with spare the dying.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

6.name: The Seeker: Astral Refuge
6.description: At 6th level, you gain the ability to step into an astral refuge. As an action, you disappear from the world for a brief moment and enter the Astral Plane, taking advantage of its timeless nature. While in your astral refuge, you can take two actions to cast spells that target only you. After using those two actions, you return to the space you occupied and your turn ends.

6.name: The Undying Light: Searing Vengeance
6.description:
Starting at 6th level, the radiant energy you channel allows you to overcome grievous injuries. When you would make a death saving throw, you can instead spring back to your feet with a burst of radiant energy. You immediately stand up (if you so choose), and you regain hit points equal to half your hit point maximum. All hostile creatures within 30 feet of you take 10 + your Charisma modifier radiant damage and are blinded until the end of your turn.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

6.name: The Hexblade: Shadow Hound
6.description:
Starting at 6th level, your shadow can split from you and transform into a hound of pure darkness. Most of the time, your shadow hound masquerades as your normal shadow. As a bonus action, you can command it to magically slip into the shadow of a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. While the shadow hound is merged in this manner, the target can’t gain the benefits of half cover or three-quarters cover against your attack rolls, and you know the distance and direction to the target even if it is hidden. The hound can’t be seen by anyone but you and those with truesight, and it is unaffected by light. The target has a vague feeling of dread while the hound is present.
As a bonus action, you can command your shadow hound to return to you. It also automatically returns to you if you and the target are on different planes of existence, if you’re incapacitated, or if dispel magic, remove curse, or similar magic is used on the target.

6.name: The Raven Queen: Soul of the Raven
6.description: At 6th level, you gain the ability to merge with your raven spirit. As a bonus action when your raven is perched on your shoulder, your body merges with your raven’s form. While merged, you become Tiny, you replace your speed with the raven’s, and you can use your action only to Dash, Disengage, Dodge, Help, Hide, or Search. During this time, you gain the benefits of your raven being perched on your shoulder. As an action, you and the raven return to normal.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation
7.description: You learn an additional eldritch invocation

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Agonizing Blast
7.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you cast eldritch blast, add your Charisma modifier to the damage it deals on a hit.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Armor of Shadows
7.description: You can cast mage armor on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Beast Speech
7.description: You can cast speak with animals at will, without expending a spell slot.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Beguiling Influence
7.description: You gain proficiency in the Deception and Persuasion skills.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Bewitching Whispers
7.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level
You can cast compulsion once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Book of Ancient Secrets
7.description:
Prerequisite: Pact of the Tome feature
You can now inscribe magical rituals in your Book of Shadows. Choose two 1st-level spells that have the ritual tag from any class's spell list. The spells appear in the book and don't count against the number of spells you know. With your Book of Shadows in hand, you can cast the chosen spells as rituals. You can't cast the spells except as rituals, unless you've learned them by some other means. You can also cast a warlock spell you know as a ritual if it has the ritual tag.
On your adventures, you can add other ritual spells to your Book of Shadows. When you find such a spell, you can add it to the book if the spell's level is equal to or less than half your warlock level (rounded up) and if you can spare the time to transcribe the spell. For each level of the spell, the transcription process takes 2 hours and costs 50 gp for the rare inks needed to inscribe it.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Devil's Sight
7.description: You can see normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 120 feet.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Dreadful Word
7.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level
You can cast confusion once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Eldritch Sight
7.description: You can cast detect magic at will, without expending a spell slot.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Eldritch Spear
7.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you cast eldritch blast, its range is 300 feet.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Eyes of the Rune Keeper
7.description: You can read all writing.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Fiendish Vigor
7.description: You can cast false life on yourself at will as a 1st-level spell, without expending a spell slot or material components.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Gaze of Two Minds
7.description: You can use your action to touch a willing humanoid and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn. As long as the creature is on the same plane of existence as you, you can use your action on subsequent turns to maintain this connection, extending the duration until the end of your next turn. While perceiving through the other creature's senses, you benefit from any special senses possessed by that creature, and you are blinded and deafened to your own surroundings.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mask of Many Faces
7.description: You can cast disguise self at will, without expending a spell slot.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mire the Mind
7.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level
You can cast slow once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Misty Visions
7.description: You can cast silent image at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: One with Shadows
7.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level
When you are in an area of dim light or darkness, you can use your action to become invisible until you move or take an action or a reaction.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Repelling Blast
7.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you hit a creature with eldritch blast, you can push the creature up to 10 feet away from you in a straight line.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Sculptor of Flesh
7.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level
You can cast polymorph once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Signs of Ill Omen
7.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level
You can cast bestow curse once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Thief of Five Fates
7.description: You can cast bane once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Thirsting Blade
7.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, Pact of the Blade feature
You can attack with your pact weapon twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Voice of the Chain Master
7.description:
Prerequisite: Pact of the Chain feature
You can communicate telepathically with your familiar and perceive through your familiar's senses as long as you are on the same plane of existence. Additionally, while perceiving through your familiar's senses, you can also speak through your familiar in your own voice, even if your familiar is normally incapable of speech.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Aspect of the Moon (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
You have gained the Maiden of the Moon’s favor. You no longer need to sleep and can’t be forced to sleep by any means. To gain the benefits of a long rest, you can spend all 8 hours doing light activity, such as reading and keeping watch.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Burning Hex (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Hexblade patron
As a bonus action, you cause a target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse to take fire damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Caiphon's Beacon (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron
The purple star Caiphon is the doom of inexperienced mariners. Those who use its deceptive light to guide their travels invariably come to ruin. You gain proficiency in the Deception and Stealth skills, and you have advantage on attack rolls against charmed creatures.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Chilling Hex (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Hexblade patron
As a bonus action, you cause frost to swirl around a target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse, dealing cold damage to each of your enemies within 5 feet of the target. The cold damage equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Chronicle of the Raven Queen (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Raven Queen patron, Pact of the Tome feature
You can place a corpse’s hand or similar appendage on your Book of Shadows and ask one question aloud. After 1 minute, the answer appears written in blood in your book. The answer is provided by the dead creature’s spirit to the best of its knowledge and is translated into a language of your choice. You must use this ability within 1 minute of a creature’s death, and a given creature can only be asked one question in this manner.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Claw of Acamar (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a black, lead flail using your Pact of the Blade feature. The flail’s head is sculpted to resemble a pair of grasping tentacles. The weapon has the reach property. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 necrotic damage to the target per spell level, and you can reduce the creature’s speed to 0 feet until the end of your next turn.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Cloak of Baalzebul (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron
As a bonus action, you can conjure a swarm of buzzing flies around you. The swarm grants you advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks but disadvantage on all other Charisma checks. In addition, a creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of you takes poison damage equal to your Charisma modifier. You can dismiss the swarm with another bonus action.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Curse Bringer (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Hexblade patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a greatsword forged from silver, with black runes etched into its blade, using your Pact of the Blade feature. If you reduce a target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse to 0 hit points with this sword, you can immediately change the target of the curse to a different creature. This change doesn’t extend the curse’s duration.
When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 slashing damage to the target per spell level, and you can reduce the creature’s speed to 0 feet until the end of your next turn.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Kiss of Mephistopheles (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, the Fiend patron, eldritch blast cantrip
You can channel the fires of Mephistopheles through your eldritch blast. When you hit a creature with that cantrip, you can cast fireball as a bonus action using a warlock spell slot. However, the spell must be centered on a creature you hit with eldritch blast.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Frost Lance (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron, eldritch blast cantrip
You draw on the gifts of the Prince of Frost to trap your enemies in ice. When you hit a creature with your eldritch blast cantrip one or more times on your turn, you can reduce that creature’s speed by 10 feet until the end of your next turn.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Gaze of Khirad (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level, the Great Old One patron
You gain the piercing gaze of the blue star Khirad. As an action, you can see through solid objects to a range of 30 feet until the end of the current turn. During that time, you perceive objects as ghostly, transparent images.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Grasp of Hadar (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron, eldritch blast cantrip
Once during your turn when you hit a creature with your eldritch blast cantrip, you can move
                    that creature in a straight line 10 feet closer to yourself.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Green Lord’s Gift (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
The Green Lord oversees a verdant realm of everlasting summer. Your soul is linked to his power. Whenever you regain hit points, you treat any dice rolled to determine the hit points you regain as having rolled their maximum value.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Improved Pact Weapon (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, Pact of the Blade feature
Any weapon you create using your Pact of the Blade feature is a +1 weapon. This invocation doesn’t affect a magic weapon you transformed into your pact weapon.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mace of Dispater (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron, Pact of the Blade feature
When you create your pact weapon as a mace, it manifests as an iron mace forged in Dis, the second of the Nine Hells. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 force damage to the target per spell level, and you can knock the target prone if it is Huge or smaller.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Moon Bow (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a longbow using your Pact of the Blade feature. When you draw back its string and fire, it creates an arrow of white wood, which vanishes after 1 minute. You have advantage on attack rolls against lycanthropes with the bow. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 radiant damage to the target per spell level.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Path of the Seeker (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Seeker patron
The Seeker bids you to travel in search of knowledge, and little can prevent you from waling this path. You ignore difficult terrain, have advantage on all checks to escape a grapple, manacles, or rope bindings, and advantage on saving throws against being paralyzed.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Raven Queen’s Blessing (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: Raven Queen patron, eldritch blast cantrip
When you score a critical hit with your eldritch blast cantrip, pick yourself or an ally you can see within 30 feet of you. The chosen creature can immediately expend a Hit Die to regain hit points equal to the roll + the creature’s Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hit point).

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Relentless Hex (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, the Hexblade patron
Your Hexblade’s Curse creates a temporary bond between you and your target. As a bonus action, you can magically teleport to a space you can see within 5 feet of the target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse. To do so, you must be able to see the target and must be within 30 feet of it.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Sea Twins’ Gift (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
The Sea Twins rule seas in the Feywild. Their gift allows you to travel through water with ease. You can breathe underwater, and you gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
You can also cast water breathing using a warlock spell slot. Once you cast it using this invocation, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Seeker’s Speech (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Seeker patron
Your quest for knowledge allows you to master any spoken language. When you complete a long rest, you can pick two languages. You gain the ability to speak, read, and write the chosen languages until you finish your next long rest.

7.name: Eldritch Invocation: Tomb of Levistus (UA)
7.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron
As a reaction when you take damage, you can entomb yourself in ice, which melts away at the end of your next turn. You gain 10 temporary hit points per warlock level, which take as much of the triggering damage as possible. You also gain vulnerability to fire damage, your speed drops to 0, and you are incapacitated. All of these effects end when the ice melts.
Once you use this invocation, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description:
When you reach 8th level you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation
9.description: You learn an additional eldritch invocation

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Agonizing Blast
9.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you cast eldritch blast, add your Charisma modifier to the damage it deals on a hit.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Armor of Shadows
9.description: You can cast mage armor on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Ascendant Step
9.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level
You can cast levitate on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Beast Speech
9.description: You can cast speak with animals at will, without expending a spell slot.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Beguiling Influence

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Otherworldly Leap
9.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level
You can cast jump on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Repelling Blast
9.description:

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Chronicle of the Raven Queen (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Raven Queen patron, Pact of the Tome feature
You can place a corpse’s hand or similar appendage on your Book of Shadows and ask one question aloud. After 1 minute, the answer appears written in blood in your book. The answer is provided by the dead creature’s spirit to the best of its knowledge and is translated into a language of your choice. You must use this ability within 1 minute of a creature’s death, and a given creature can only be asked one question in this manner.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Claw of Acamar (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a black, lead flail using your Pact of the Blade feature. The flail’s head is sculpted to resemble a pair of grasping tentacles. The weapon has the reach property. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 necrotic damage to the target per spell level, and you can reduce the creature’s speed to 0 feet until the end of your next turn.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Cloak of Baalzebul (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron
As a bonus action, you can conjure a swarm of buzzing flies around you. The swarm grants you advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks but disadvantage on all other Charisma checks. In addition, a creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of you takes poison damage equal to your Charisma modifier. You can dismiss the swarm with another bonus action.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Curse Bringer (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Hexblade patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a greatsword forged from silver, with black runes etched into its blade, using your Pact of the Blade feature. If you reduce a target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse to 0 hit points with this sword, you can immediately change the target of the curse to a different creature. This change doesn’t extend the curse’s duration.
When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 slashing damage to the target per spell level, and you can reduce the creature’s speed to 0 feet until the end of your next turn.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Kiss of Mephistopheles (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, the Fiend patron, eldritch blast cantrip
You can channel the fires of Mephistopheles through your eldritch blast. When you hit a creature with that cantrip, you can cast fireball as a bonus action using a warlock spell slot. However, the spell must be centered on a creature you hit with eldritch blast.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Frost Lance (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron, eldritch blast cantrip
You draw on the gifts of the Prince of Frost to trap your enemies in ice. When you hit a creature with your eldritch blast cantrip one or more times on your turn, you can reduce that creature’s speed by 10 feet until the end of your next turn.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Gaze of Khirad (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level, the Great Old One patron
You gain the piercing gaze of the blue star Khirad. As an action, you can see through solid objects to a range of 30 feet until the end of the current turn. During that time, you perceive objects as ghostly, transparent images.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Grasp of Hadar (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron, eldritch blast cantrip
Once during your turn when you hit a creature with your eldritch blast cantrip, you can move
                    that creature in a straight line 10 feet closer to yourself.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Green Lord’s Gift (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
The Green Lord oversees a verdant realm of everlasting summer. Your soul is linked to his power. Whenever you regain hit points, you treat any dice rolled to determine the hit points you regain as having rolled their maximum value.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Improved Pact Weapon (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, Pact of the Blade feature
Any weapon you create using your Pact of the Blade feature is a +1 weapon. This invocation doesn’t affect a magic weapon you transformed into your pact weapon.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mace of Dispater (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron, Pact of the Blade feature
When you create your pact weapon as a mace, it manifests as an iron mace forged in Dis, the second of the Nine Hells. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 force damage to the target per spell level, and you can knock the target prone if it is Huge or smaller.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Moon Bow (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a longbow using your Pact of the Blade feature. When you draw back its string and fire, it creates an arrow of white wood, which vanishes after 1 minute. You have advantage on attack rolls against lycanthropes with the bow. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 radiant damage to the target per spell level.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Path of the Seeker (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Seeker patron
The Seeker bids you to travel in search of knowledge, and little can prevent you from waling this path. You ignore difficult terrain, have advantage on all checks to escape a grapple, manacles, or rope bindings, and advantage on saving throws against being paralyzed.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Raven Queen’s Blessing (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: Raven Queen patron, eldritch blast cantrip
When you score a critical hit with your eldritch blast cantrip, pick yourself or an ally you can see within 30 feet of you. The chosen creature can immediately expend a Hit Die to regain hit points equal to the roll + the creature’s Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hit point).

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Relentless Hex (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, the Hexblade patron
Your Hexblade’s Curse creates a temporary bond between you and your target. As a bonus action, you can magically teleport to a space you can see within 5 feet of the target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse. To do so, you must be able to see the target and must be within 30 feet of it.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Sea Twins’ Gift (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
The Sea Twins rule seas in the Feywild. Their gift allows you to travel through water with ease. You can breathe underwater, and you gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
You can also cast water breathing using a warlock spell slot. Once you cast it using this invocation, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Seeker’s Speech (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Seeker patron
Your quest for knowledge allows you to master any spoken language. When you complete a long rest, you can pick two languages. You gain the ability to speak, read, and write the chosen languages until you finish your next long rest.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Superior Pact Weapon (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level, Pact of the Blade feature
Any weapon you create using your Pact of the Blade feature is a +2 weapon. This invocation doesn’t affect a magic weapon you transformed into your pact weapon.

9.name: Eldritch Invocation: Tomb of Levistus (UA)
9.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron
As a reaction when you take damage, you can entomb yourself in ice, which melts away at the end of your next turn. You gain 10 temporary hit points per warlock level, which take as much of the triggering damage as possible. You also gain vulnerability to fire damage, your speed drops to 0, and you are incapacitated. All of these effects end when the ice melts.
Once you use this invocation, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

10.name: The Archfey: Beguiling Defenses
10.description: Beginning at 10th level, your patron teaches you how to turn the mind-affecting magic of your enemies against them. You are immune to being charmed, and when another creature attempts to charm you, you can use your reaction to attempt to turn the charm back on that creature. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your warlock spell save DC or be charmed by you for 1 minute or until the creature takes any damage.

10.name: The Fiend: Fiendish Resilience
10.description: Starting at 10th level, you can choose one damage type when you finish a short or long rest. You gain resistance to that damage type until you choose a different one with this feature. Damage from magical weapons or silver weapons ignores this resistance.

10.name: The Great Old One: Thought Shield
10.description: Starting at 10th level, your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means unless you allow it. You also have resistance to psychic damage, and whenever a creature deals psychic damage to you, that creature takes the same amount of damage that you do.

10.name: The Undying: Undying Nature
10.description:
Beginning at 10th level, you can hold your breath indefinitely, and you don't require food, water, or sleep, although you still require rest to reduce exhaustion and still benefit from finishing short and long rests.
In addition, you age at a slower rate. For every 10 years that pass, your body ages only 1 year, and you are immune to being magically aged.

10.name: The Seeker: Far Wanderer
10.description: At 10th level, you no longer need to breathe, and you gain resistance to fire damage and cold damage.

10.name: The Undying Light: Radiant Resilience
10.description: Starting at 10th level, you gain temporary hit points whenever you finish a long or short rest. These temporary hit points equal your warlock level + your Charisma modifier. Additionally, choose up to five creatures you can see at the end of your rest. Those creatures gain temporary hit points equal to half your warlock level + your Charisma modifier.

10.name: The Hexblade: Armor of Hexes
10.description: At 10th level, your hex grows more powerful. If the target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse hits you with an attack roll, roll a d6. On a 4 or higher, the attack instead misses you.

10.name: The Raven Queen: Raven's Shield
10.description: At 10th level, the Raven Queen grants you a protective blessing. You gain advantage on death saving throws, immunity to the frightened condition, and resistance to necrotic damage.

11.name: Mystic Arcanum (6th level)
11.description:
At 11th level, your patron bestows upon you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one 6th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum.
You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.
At higher levels, you gain more warlock spells of your choice that can be cast in this way: one 7th-level spell at 13th level, one 8th-level spell at 15th level, and one 9th-level spell at 17th level. You regain all uses of your Mystic A rcanum when you finish a long rest.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description:
When you reach 12th level you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation
12.description: You learn an additional eldritch invocation

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Agonizing Blast
12.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you cast eldritch blast, add your Charisma modifier to the damage it deals on a hit.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Armor of Shadows
12.description: You can cast mage armor on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Ascendant Step
12.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level
You can cast levitate on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Beast Speech
12.description: You can cast speak with animals at will, without expending a spell slot.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Beguiling Influence
12.description: You gain proficiency in the Deception and Persuasion skills.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Bewitching Whispers
12.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level
You can cast compulsion once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Book of Ancient Secrets
12.description:
Prerequisite: Pact of the Tome feature
You can now inscribe magical rituals in your Book of Shadows. Choose two 1st-level spells that have the ritual tag from any class's spell list. The spells appear in the book and don't count against the number of spells you know. With your Book of Shadows in hand, you can cast the chosen spells as rituals. You can't cast the spells except as rituals, unless you've learned them by some other means. You can also cast a warlock spell you know as a ritual if it has the ritual tag.
On your adventures, you can add other ritual spells to your Book of Shadows. When you find such a spell, you can add it to the book if the spell's level is equal to or less than half your warlock level (rounded up) and if you can spare the time to transcribe the spell. For each level of the spell, the transcription process takes 2 hours and costs 50 gp for the rare inks needed to inscribe it.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Devil's Sight
12.description: You can see normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 120 feet.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Dreadful Word
12.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level
You can cast confusion once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Eldritch Sight
12.description: You can cast detect magic at will, without expending a spell slot.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Eldritch Spear
12.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you cast eldritch blast, its range is 300 feet.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Eyes of the Rune Keeper
12.description: You can read all writing.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Fiendish Vigor
12.description: You can cast false life on yourself at will as a 1st-level spell, without expending a spell slot or material components.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Gaze of Two Minds
12.description: You can use your action to touch a willing humanoid and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn. As long as the creature is on the same plane of existence as you, you can use your action on subsequent turns to maintain this connection, extending the duration until the end of your next turn. While perceiving through the other creature's senses, you benefit from any special senses possessed by that creature, and you are blinded and deafened to your own surroundings.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Lifedrinker
12.description:
Prerequisite: 12th level
When you hit a creature with your pact weapon, the creature takes extra necrotic damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1).

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mask of Many Faces
12.description: You can cast disguise self at will, without expending a spell slot.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Minions of Chaos
12.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level
You can cast conjure elemental once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mire the Mind
12.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level
You can cast slow once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Misty Visions
12.description: You can cast silent image at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: One with Shadows
12.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level
When you are in an area of dim light or darkness, you can use your action to become invisible until you move or take an action or a reaction.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Otherworldly Leap
12.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level
You can cast jump on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Repelling Blast
12.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you hit a creature with eldritch blast, you can push the creature up to 10 feet away from you in a straight line.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Sculptor of Flesh
12.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level
You can cast polymorph once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Signs of Ill Omen
12.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level
You can cast bestow curse once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Thief of Five Fates
12.description: You can cast bane once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Thirsting Blade
12.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, Pact of the Blade feature
You can attack with your pact weapon twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Voice of the Chain Master
12.description:
Prerequisite: Pact of the Chain feature
You can communicate telepathically with your familiar and perceive through your familiar's senses as long as you are on the same plane of existence. Additionally, while perceiving through your familiar's senses, you can also speak through your familiar in your own voice, even if your familiar is normally incapable of speech.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Whispers of the Grave
12.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level
You can cast speak with dead at will, without expending a spell slot.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Aspect of the Moon (UA)
12.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
You have gained the Maiden of the Moon’s favor. You no longer need to sleep and can’t be forced to sleep by any means. To gain the benefits of a long rest, you can spend all 8 hours doing light activity, such as reading and keeping watch.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Burning Hex (UA)
12.description:
Prerequisite: The Hexblade patron
As a bonus action, you cause a target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse to take fire damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Caiphon's Beacon (UA)
12.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron
The purple star Caiphon is the doom of inexperienced mariners. Those who use its deceptive light to guide their travels invariably come to ruin. You gain proficiency in the Deception and Stealth skills, and you have advantage on attack rolls against charmed creatures.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Chilling Hex (UA)
12.description:
Prerequisite: The Hexblade patron
As a bonus action, you cause frost to swirl around a target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse, dealing cold damage to each of your enemies within 5 feet of the target. The cold damage equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Chronicle of the Raven Queen (UA)
12.description:
Prerequisite: The Raven Queen patron, Pact of the Tome feature
You can place a corpse’s hand or similar appendage on your Book of Shadows and ask one question aloud. After 1 minute, the answer appears written in blood in your book. The answer is provided by the dead creature’s spirit to the best of its knowledge and is translated into a language of your choice. You must use this ability within 1 minute of a creature’s death, and a given creature can only be asked one question in this manner.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Claw of Acamar (UA)
12.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a black, lead flail using your Pact of the Blade feature. The flail’s head is sculpted to resemble a pair of grasping tentacles. The weapon has the reach property. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 necrotic damage to the target per spell level, and you can reduce the creature’s speed to 0 feet until the end of your next turn.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Cloak of Baalzebul (UA)
12.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron
As a bonus action, you can conjure a swarm of buzzing flies around you. The swarm grants you advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks but disadvantage on all other Charisma checks. 12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Curse Bringer (UA)
12.description:

You can create a greatsword forged from silver, with black runes etched into its blade, using your Pact of the Blade feature. 12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Kiss of Mephistopheles (UA)
12.description:

You can channel the fires of Mephistopheles through your eldritch blast. When you hit a creature with that cantrip, you can cast fireball as a bonus action using a warlock spell slot. However, the spell must be centered on a creature you hit with eldritch blast.

12.description:

12.description:

                    that creature in a straight line 10 feet closer to yourself.

12.description:

12.description:

12.description:

12.description:

You can also cast water breathing using a warlock spell slot. Once you cast it using this invocation, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

12.description:

12.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level, Pact of the Blade feature
Any weapon you create using your Pact of the Blade feature is a +2 weapon. This invocation doesn’t affect a magic weapon you transformed into your pact weapon.

12.name: Eldritch Invocation: Tomb of Levistus (UA)
12.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron
As a reaction when you take damage, you can entomb yourself in ice, which melts away at the end of your next turn. You gain 10 temporary hit points per warlock level, which take as much of the triggering damage as possible. You also gain vulnerability to fire damage, your speed drops to 0, and you are incapacitated. All of these effects end when the ice melts.
Once you use this invocation, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

13.name: Mystic Arcanum (7th level)
13.description:
At 13th level, your patron bestows upon you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one 7th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum.
You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

14.name: The Archfey: Dark Delirium
14.description:
Starting at 14th level, you can plunge a creature into an illusory realm. As an action, choose a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you. It must make a Wisdom saving throw against your warlock spell save DC. On a failed save, it is charmed or frightened by you (your choice) for 1 minute or until your concentration is broken (as if you are concentrating on a spell). This effect ends early if the creature takes any damage.
Until this illusion ends, the creature thinks it is lost in a misty realm, the appearance of which you choose. The creature can see and hear only itself, you, and the illusion.
You must finish a short or long rest before you can use this feature again.

14.name: The Fiend: Hurl Through Hell
14.description:
Starting at 14th level, when you hit a creature with an attack, you can use this feature to instantly transport the target through the lower planes. The creature disappears and hurtles through a nightmare landscape.
At the end of your next turn, the target returns to the space it previously occupied, or the nearest unoccupied space. If the target is not a fiend, it takes 10d 10 psychic damage as it reels from its horrific experience.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

14.name: The Great Old One: Create Thrall
14.description:
At 14th level, you gain the ability to infect a humanoid's mind with the alien magic of your patron. You can use your action to touch an incapacitated humanoid. That creature is then charmed by you until a remove curse spell is cast on it, the charmed condition is removed from it, or you use this feature again.
You can communicate telepathically with the charmed creature as long as the two of you are on the same plane of existence.

14.name: The Undying: Indestructible Life
14.description:
When you reach 14th level, you partake some of the true secrets of the Undying. On your turn, you can use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d8 + your warlock level. Additionally, if you put a severed body part of yours back in place when you use this feature, the part reattaches.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

14.name: The Seeker: Astral Sequestration
14.description:
Starting at 14th level, you gain the ability to sequester yourself and your allies on the Astral Plane.
By performing a special ritual over the course of 5 minutes, you shift yourself and up to ten willing creatures you can see to the Astral Plane. You and those creatures gain the benefits of a short rest while sequestered on the Astral Plane. You then return to the spaces you all occupied when you used this ability, with no time having passed in the world.
During this short rest, you and the creatures you sequester can make use of any options available during a rest that affect only you and the creatures you sequester.
Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again until you complete a long rest.

14.name: The Undying Light: Healing Light
14.description:
At 14th level, you gain the ability to channel the Undying Light to heal yourself and other creatures. As a bonus action, you can touch a creature and heal it. With each touch, a creature regains from 1d6 to 5d6 hit points (your choice). You have a total pool of 15d6 you can expend. Subtract the dice you use with each touch from that total.
You regain all expended dice from your pool when you finish a long rest.

14.name: The Hexblade: Master of Hexes
14.description: Starting at 14th level, you can use your Hexblade’s Curse again without resting, but when you apply it to a new target, the curse immediately ends on the previous target.

14.name: The Raven Queen: Queen's Right Hand
14.description: Starting at 14th level, you can channel the Raven Queen’s power to slay a creature. You can cast finger of death. After you cast the spell with this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation
15.description: You learn an additional eldritch invocation

15.name: Mystic Arcanum (8th level)
15.description:
At 15th level, your patron bestows upon you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one 8th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum.
You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Agonizing Blast
15.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you cast eldritch blast, add your Charisma modifier to the damage it deals on a hit.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Armor of Shadows
15.description: You can cast mage armor on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Ascendant Step
15.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level
You can cast levitate on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Beast Speech
15.description: You can cast speak with animals at will, without expending a spell slot.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Beguiling Influence
15.description: You gain proficiency in the Deception and Persuasion skills.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Bewitching Whispers
15.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level
You can cast compulsion once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Book of Ancient Secrets
15.description:
Prerequisite: Pact of the Tome feature
You can now inscribe magical rituals in your Book of Shadows. Choose two 1st-level spells that have the ritual tag from any class's spell list. The spells appear in the book and don't count against the number of spells you know. With your Book of Shadows in hand, you can cast the chosen spells as rituals. You can't cast the spells except as rituals, unless you've learned them by some other means. You can also cast a warlock spell you know as a ritual if it has the ritual tag.
On your adventures, you can add other ritual spells to your Book of Shadows. When you find such a spell, you can add it to the book if the spell's level is equal to or less than half your warlock level (rounded up) and if you can spare the time to transcribe the spell. For each level of the spell, the transcription process takes 2 hours and costs 50 gp for the rare inks needed to inscribe it.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Chains of Carceri
15.description:
Prerequisite: 15th level, Pact of the Chain feature
You can cast hold monster at will—targeting a celestial, fiend, or elemental—without expending a spell slot or material components. You must finish a long rest before you can use this invocation on the same creature again.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Devil's Sight
15.description: You can see normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 120 feet.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Dreadful Word
15.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level
You can cast confusion once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Eldritch Sight
15.description: You can cast detect magic at will, without expending a spell slot.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Eldritch Spear
15.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you cast eldritch blast, its range is 300 feet.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Eyes of the Rune Keeper
15.description: You can read all writing.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Fiendish Vigor
15.description: You can cast false life on yourself at will as a 1st-level spell, without expending a spell slot or material components.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Gaze of Two Minds
15.description: You can use your action to touch a willing humanoid and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn. As long as the creature is on the same plane of existence as you, you can use your action on subsequent turns to maintain this connection, extending the duration until the end of your next turn. While perceiving through the other creature's senses, you benefit from any special senses possessed by that creature, and you are blinded and deafened to your own surroundings.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Lifedrinker
15.description:
Prerequisite: 12th level
When you hit a creature with your pact weapon, the creature takes extra necrotic damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1).

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mask of Many Faces
15.description: You can cast disguise self at will, without expending a spell slot.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Master of Myriad Forms
15.description:
Prerequisite: 15th level
You can cast alter self at will, without expending a spell slot.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Minions of Chaos
15.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level
You can cast conjure elemental once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mire the Mind
15.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level
You can cast slow once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Misty Visions
15.description: You can cast silent image at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: One with Shadows
15.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level
When you are in an area of dim light or darkness, you can use your action to become invisible until you move or take an action or a reaction.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Otherworldly Leap
15.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level
You can cast jump on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Repelling Blast
15.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you hit a creature with eldritch blast, you can push the creature up to 10 feet away from you in a straight line.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Sculptor of Flesh
15.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level
You can cast polymorph once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Signs of Ill Omen
15.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level
You can cast bestow curse once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Thief of Five Fates
15.description: You can cast bane once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Thirsting Blade
15.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, Pact of the Blade feature
You can attack with your pact weapon twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Visions of Distant Realms
15.description:
Prerequisite: 15th level
You can cast arcane eye at will, without expending a spell slot.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Voice of the Chain Master
15.description:
Prerequisite: Pact of the Chain feature
You can communicate telepathically with your familiar and perceive through your familiar's senses as long as you are on the same plane of existence. Additionally, while perceiving through your familiar's senses, you can also speak through your familiar in your own voice, even if your familiar is normally incapable of speech.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Whispers of the Grave
15.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level
You can cast speak with dead at will, without expending a spell slot.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Witch Sight
15.description:
Prerequisite: 15th level
You can see the true form of any shapechanger or creature concealed by illusion or transmutation magic while the creature is within 30 feet of you and within line of sight.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Aspect of the Moon (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
You have gained the Maiden of the Moon’s favor. You no longer need to sleep and can’t be forced to sleep by any means. To gain the benefits of a long rest, you can spend all 8 hours doing light activity, such as reading and keeping watch.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Burning Hex (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Hexblade patron
As a bonus action, you cause a target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse to take fire damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Caiphon's Beacon (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron
The purple star Caiphon is the doom of inexperienced mariners. Those who use its deceptive light to guide their travels invariably come to ruin. You gain proficiency in the Deception and Stealth skills, and you have advantage on attack rolls against charmed creatures.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Chilling Hex (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Hexblade patron
As a bonus action, you cause frost to swirl around a target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse, dealing cold damage to each of your enemies within 5 feet of the target. The cold damage equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Chronicle of the Raven Queen (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Raven Queen patron, Pact of the Tome feature
You can place a corpse’s hand or similar appendage on your Book of Shadows and ask one question aloud. After 1 minute, the answer appears written in blood in your book. The answer is provided by the dead creature’s spirit to the best of its knowledge and is translated into a language of your choice. You must use this ability within 1 minute of a creature’s death, and a given creature can only be asked one question in this manner.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Claw of Acamar (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a black, lead flail using your Pact of the Blade feature. The flail’s head is sculpted to resemble a pair of grasping tentacles. The weapon has the reach property. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 necrotic damage to the target per spell level, and you can reduce the creature’s speed to 0 feet until the end of your next turn.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Cloak of Baalzebul (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron
As a bonus action, you can conjure a swarm of buzzing flies around you. The swarm grants you advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks but disadvantage on all other Charisma checks. In addition, a creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of you takes poison damage equal to your Charisma modifier. You can dismiss the swarm with another bonus action.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Curse Bringer (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Hexblade patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a greatsword forged from silver, with black runes etched into its blade, using your Pact of the Blade feature. If you reduce a target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse to 0 hit points with this sword, you can immediately change the target of the curse to a different creature. This change doesn’t extend the curse’s duration.
When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 slashing damage to the target per spell level, and you can reduce the creature’s speed to 0 feet until the end of your next turn.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Kiss of Mephistopheles (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, the Fiend patron, eldritch blast cantrip
You can channel the fires of Mephistopheles through your eldritch blast. When you hit a creature with that cantrip, you can cast fireball as a bonus action using a warlock spell slot. However, the spell must be centered on a creature you hit with eldritch blast.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Frost Lance (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron, eldritch blast cantrip
You draw on the gifts of the Prince of Frost to trap your enemies in ice. When you hit a creature with your eldritch blast cantrip one or more times on your turn, you can reduce that creature’s speed by 10 feet until the end of your next turn.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Gaze of Khirad (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: 7th level, the Great Old One patron
You gain the piercing gaze of the blue star Khirad. As an action, you can see through solid objects to a range of 30 feet until the end of the current turn. During that time, you perceive objects as ghostly, transparent images.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Grasp of Hadar (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Great Old One patron, eldritch blast cantrip
Once during your turn when you hit a creature with your eldritch blast cantrip, you can move
                    that creature in a straight line 10 feet closer to yourself.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Green Lord’s Gift (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
The Green Lord oversees a verdant realm of everlasting summer. Your soul is linked to his power. Whenever you regain hit points, you treat any dice rolled to determine the hit points you regain as having rolled their maximum value.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Improved Pact Weapon (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, Pact of the Blade feature
Any weapon you create using your Pact of the Blade feature is a +1 weapon. This invocation doesn’t affect a magic weapon you transformed into your pact weapon.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mace of Dispater (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron, Pact of the Blade feature
When you create your pact weapon as a mace, it manifests as an iron mace forged in Dis, the second of the Nine Hells. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 force damage to the target per spell level, and you can knock the target prone if it is Huge or smaller.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Moon Bow (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron, Pact of the Blade feature
You can create a longbow using your Pact of the Blade feature. When you draw back its string and fire, it creates an arrow of white wood, which vanishes after 1 minute. You have advantage on attack rolls against lycanthropes with the bow. When you hit a creature with it, you can expend a spell slot to deal an additional 2d8 radiant damage to the target per spell level.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Path of the Seeker (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Seeker patron
The Seeker bids you to travel in search of knowledge, and little can prevent you from waling this path. You ignore difficult terrain, have advantage on all checks to escape a grapple, manacles, or rope bindings, and advantage on saving throws against being paralyzed.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Raven Queen’s Blessing (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: Raven Queen patron, eldritch blast cantrip
When you score a critical hit with your eldritch blast cantrip, pick yourself or an ally you can see within 30 feet of you. The chosen creature can immediately expend a Hit Die to regain hit points equal to the roll + the creature’s Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hit point).

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Relentless Hex (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: 5th level, the Hexblade patron
Your Hexblade’s Curse creates a temporary bond between you and your target. As a bonus action, you can magically teleport to a space you can see within 5 feet of the target cursed by your Hexblade’s Curse. To do so, you must be able to see the target and must be within 30 feet of it.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Sea Twins’ Gift (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Archfey patron
The Sea Twins rule seas in the Feywild. Their gift allows you to travel through water with ease. You can breathe underwater, and you gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
You can also cast water breathing using a warlock spell slot. Once you cast it using this invocation, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Seeker’s Speech (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Seeker patron
Your quest for knowledge allows you to master any spoken language. When you complete a long rest, you can pick two languages. You gain the ability to speak, read, and write the chosen languages until you finish your next long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Superior Pact Weapon (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: 9th level, Pact of the Blade feature
Any weapon you create using your Pact of the Blade feature is a +2 weapon. This invocation doesn’t affect a magic weapon you transformed into your pact weapon.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Tomb of Levistus (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: The Fiend patron
As a reaction when you take damage, you can entomb yourself in ice, which melts away at the end of your next turn. You gain 10 temporary hit points per warlock level, which take as much of the triggering damage as possible. You also gain vulnerability to fire damage, your speed drops to 0, and you are incapacitated. All of these effects end when the ice melts.
Once you use this invocation, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocation: Ultimate Pact Weapon (UA)
15.description:
Prerequisite: 15th level, Pact of the Blade feature
Any weapon you create using your Pact of the Blade feature is a +3 weapon. This invocation doesn’t affect a magic weapon you transformed into your pact weapon.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description:
When you reach 16th level you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

17.name: Mystic Arcanum (9th level)
17.description:
At 17th level, your patron bestows upon you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one 9th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum.
You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

18.name: Eldritch Invocation
18.description: You learn an additional eldritch invocation

18.name: Eldritch Invocation: Agonizing Blast
18.description:
Prerequisite: eldritch blast cantrip
When you cast eldritch blast, add your Charisma modifier to the damage it deals on a hit.

18.name: Eldritch Invocation: Armor of Shadows
18.description: You can cast mage armor on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.
